    -- Prove that f is injective
    have h₁ : Function.Injective (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap := by
      -- Since ker_v L is a ring homomorphism, it is injective if and only if its kernel is zero
      have h₂ : Function.Injective (ker_v L : 𝒪[L] →+* K) := by
        -- The kernel of ker_v L is zero, hence it is injective
        exact (ker_v L).injective
      -- Since (ker_v L : 𝒪[L] →+* K) is injective, its associated linear map is also injective
      exact LinearMap.injective_iff_ker_eq_bot.mp (by
        simpa [LinearMap.ker] using h₂)
    -- Since the linear map associated to f is injective, f is injective
    have h₂ : Function.Injective (f.hom : X₁ →ₗ[ℤ] X₂) := by
      simpa [f] using h₁
    -- Therefore, f is injective
    exact h₂
    ,
    by
    -- Prove that g is surjective
    have h₁ : Function.Surjective (v L).toIntLinearMap := by
      -- Since v L is a ring homomorphism, it is surjective if and only if its image is the entire codomain
      have h₂ : Function.Surjective (v L : L →+* ℤ) := by
        -- The image of v L is the entire ℤ, hence it is surjective
        exact (v L).surjective
      -- Since (v L : L →+* ℤ) is surjective, its associated linear map is also surjective
      exact LinearMap.surjective_iff_range_eq_top.mp (by
        simpa [LinearMap.range] using h₂)
    -- Since the linear map associated to g is surjective, g is surjective
    have h₂ : Function.Surjective (g.hom : X₂ →ₗ[ℤ] X₃) := by
      simpa [g] using h₁
    -- Therefore, g is surjective
    exact h₂
  ⟩

In Lean, the `MulSemiringAction G L` is an action of `G` as semiring endomorphisms of `L`. 

Thus, for `g : G`, `g • (u * v) = (g • u) * (g • v)`, and `g • 1 = 1`.
